content:
Character: Ethan Cole
Perception: Ethan is the teenager who reads at the coffee shop — thin, wide-eyed, always buried in a novel too old for his age, which is how I know he's serious. He once asked me about Thucydides with a nervousness that was endearing — he actually listened to my answer. He has the look of a young writer: watchful, self-conscious, privately intense. I think he sees me as an academic curiosity. I see him as someone at the beginning of a journey I recognize — the painful, necessary stage where you love words more than you trust your own. I know he lives in the building with his mother.
